rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO MARIN COUNTY** CONS. OF DONALD PAUL LERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/2021. 1) Page 8 paragraph 26 of the petition alleges there are two transaction of June 4, 2012 and June 16, 2012 for $5,000.00. Examiner requested a bank statement from June 2012 showing the disbursements. The provided bank statemnt shows a deposit for $10,000.01 on 06/04/2012 and $5,000.00 on 6/16/2012 it does not match what is in the account and it appears the account must be amended. 2) Petitioner has not filed a response to this issue. Please provide legal authority for including "assumed" amounts in the account. 3) In the 05/28/2021 Amendment petitioner added entry 3/26/2013 for $100.00 for reimbrusement to payee for transportation and lunch for Conservatee. Please file a receipt for the entry dated March 26, 2013 for $100.00. If no receipt is available please provide the legal basis for the court allowing the entry. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CADIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/21. (No earlier availability.) The Second Amended First And Final Account, etc., was filed on 5/27/21. Notice was reportedly mailed on 5/26/21, before the pleading was filed. This was noted in the tentative rulings for the last continuance. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The two I&As filed in this estate total $695,994.42, not $700,603.82. The Summary of Account makes no sense and the accounting, such as it is in its incomplete state, also does not make sense, however, there are a number of "expenses" shown on Schedule B that will need further clarification, such as "estate expenses" without any itemization and payments to the attorney without a Court order. Counsel is directed to review Probate Code Sections 1061 - 1064 to see how an accounting should be prepared. The accounting period must be DATES, not just "to the present." None of the Exhibits referenced in the pleading appears to be correct after Exhibit A. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or BENEFICIARY has PREVIOUSLY been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (Paragraph 7 of the pleading, furthermore, has conflicting allegations.) Is the executor requesting a reserve? Paragraphs 11 and 13 appear to suggest it, but there is nothing in the prayer. The prayer is also missing any request for attorney fees. Exhibit H is an unsigned and un-endorsed filed copy of a proposed order; it is meaningless. Exhibit I is incomplete; it begins on page 2. Why hasn't the executor paid the three creditors already (although no Allowance or Rejection of those three claims has been filed) so that she doesn't have to keep serving them notice? This Examiner is not an interested party; notice does not need to be given to Court staff. Nor is information provided by an Examiner the legal authority for denying a late-filed claim by an unappointed party seeking a portion of statutory fees. Courtesy (endorsed filed) copies of the amendment and a correct and complete proof of service showing that notice of the correct pleadings was given must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/16/21. It appears that a full, complete, and accurate THIRD AMENDED FIRST AND FINAL ACCOUNT, etc., must be filed that includes an accounting that fully conforms with the Probate Code. Notice must be given of the Third Amended petition after it is filed.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition has been filed yet either to distribute the estate or to request additional time. (The "status report" filed on 12/17/20 was not signed and verified by the executor. [Prob. C. Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] There was no prayer in that "status report" and no proof of service has been filed to indicate that notice of it was given to the other beneficiaries.) NB: Nothing has been filed to indicate that the parties entitled to proceeds from the sale of the real properties have agreed not to sell. The Court may suspend (or remove) the executor. [Prob. C. Sects. 8505 and 12204.] Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT S. MELENDY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The Court cannot find and order what is not set forth in a verified pleading. The terms of the testamentary trust must be set forth in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the executor. The supplement must also address the following issues: 1. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. 2. The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code Sect. 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, which must be set forth verbatim in the (proposed) order that is submitted to the Court. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/16/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARION JENKINS AND GANAIL JENKINS LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Instructing Trustee To Place Property In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required (although courtesy copies of the second supplement filed on 5/20/21 and proof of service filed on 5/24/21 have not yet been received by the assigned Examiner). Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. The Court to determine if this is the proper venue, when the successor trustee resides in Oakland. Merely having a Probate estate opened here for one of the settlors does not mean that the trust is being administered here. [Probate Code Sections 17002(a) and 17005(a)(1).] Other issues that will need to be addressed: 1) A second supplement was just filed on 5/20/21, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. [CRC 7.53(b).] 2) Two of the accounts collected by the executor of Marion's will are now no longer part of this petition, which amounts to an amendment to the petition, which would require notice. [CRC 7.3(4) and 7.53(a).] 3) Several of the beneficiaries' names were apparently misspelled in the trust instrument, because the beneficiaries listed in the petition and service lists are not all as identified in the trust, but the petition does not address this inconsistency.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUEN MOON LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The petition alleged the will was self-proving under Probate Code 8220. Examiner requested an explanation and legal authority for why the petition alleged the Will is self-proving. No explanation or legal authority filed. Petitioner filed Proof of Subscribing Witness Statements by Tiffany Brooks and Debbie Yancey. 2) Court to determine amount of bond as Petitioner is a nonresident of California. Examiner requested a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. Instead of providing detailed anticipated liabilities a Supplement was filed alleging $20,000.00 in bond was erroneously filed prior to being appointed and Petitioner assets the claims against the estate will be less than $20,000.00. 3) Examiner requested an explanation for petitioner Donny H. Lau being an interpreter/translator for Decedent's Will. Supplement filed 06/02/2021.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/2021. It appears there are also filings in the que still to be processed. 1) Issues in Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition: a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Based on the other petition filed there appear to be other heirs that have not received notice. 2) Issues In Jonathan Campbell's Petition: a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Examiner does not have a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENA BEE LIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/7/2021. 1) Petitioner must submit a revised proposed order that includes an attachment 5a and attachment 7a. Do not attach or include any deeds to the proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/28/21 for failure to address all issues in Examiner's Notes. All documents required must be filed and provided to Room 202 at least 2 weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF JAMES MCGHIE ALLAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.
